D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Sobel — Gradient-Magnitude Edge Detection

3×3 Sobel kernels approximate ∂I/∂x and ∂I/∂y; |∇I| = √(Gx² + Gy²) gives an edge map — used in Canny, RANSAC, and a thousand vision pipelines

Sobel kernels have a built-in 1-2-1 smoothing component that suppresses noise while measuring local derivative. The two outputs Gx, Gy give per-pixel gradient direction (atan2 Gy Gx) as well as magnitude. Cheaper alternative: Prewitt (uniform 1-1-1 weighting, more noise).

Slide gradient gain to amplify edges; toggle between Gx, Gy, magnitude.
